author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter XIV. 10–14.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give close attention, and try to understand, beloved; for while it is we who speak it is He Himself who never withdraweth His presence from us who is our Teacher.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord saith, what you have just heard read, “The words that I speak unto you, I speak not of myself: but the Father, that dwelleth in me, He doeth the works.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even His words, then, are works?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For surely he that edifies a neighbor by what he says, works a good work.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what mean the words, “I speak not of myself,” but, I who speak am not of myself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence He attributes what He does to Him, of whom He, that doeth them, is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Father is not God [as born, etc.] of any one else, while the Son is God, as equal, indeed, to the Father, but [as born] of God the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore the former is God, but not of God; and the Light, but not of light: whereas the latter is God of God, Light of Light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in connection with these two clauses,—the one where it is said, “I speak not of myself;” and the other, which runs, “but the Father that dwelleth in me, He doeth the works,”—we are opposed by two different classes of heretics, who, by each of them holding only to one clause, run off, not in one, but opposite directions, and wander far from the pathway of truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For instance, the Arians say, See here, the Son is not equal to the Father, He speaketh not of Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Sabellians, or Patripassians, on the other hand, say, See, He who is the Father is also the Son; for what else is this, “The Father that dwelleth in me, He doeth the works,” but I that do them dwell in myself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You make contrary assertions, and that not only in the sense that any one thing is false, that is, contrary to truth, but in this also, when two things that are both false contradict one another.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In your wanderings you have taken opposite directions; midway between the two is the path you have left.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You are a far longer distance apart from each other than from the very way you have both forsaken.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Come hither, you from the one side, and you from the other: pass not across, the one to the other, but come from both sides to us, and make this the place of your mutual meeting.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye Sabellians, acknowledge the Being you overlook; Arians, set Him whom you subordinate in His place of equality, and you will both be walking with us in the pathway of truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For you have grounds on both sides that make mutual admonition a duty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen, Sabellian: so far is the Son from being the same as the Father, and so truly is He another, that the Arian maintains His inferiority to the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen, Arian: so truly is the Son equal to the Father, that the Sabellian declares Him to be identical with the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do thou restore the personality thou hast abstracted, and thou, the full dignity thou hast lowered, and both of you stand together on the same ground as ourselves: because the one of you [who has been an Arian], for the conviction of the Sabellian, never lets out of sight the personality of Him who is distinct from the Father, and the other [who has been a Sabellian] takes care, for the conviction of the Arian, of not impairing the dignity of Him who is equal with the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For to both of you He cries, “I and my Father are one.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When He says “one,” let the Arians listen; when He says, “we are,” let the Sabellians give heed, and no longer continue in the folly of denying, the one, His equality [with the Father], the other, His distinct personality.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, in saying, “The words that I speak unto you, I speak not of myself,” He is thereby accounted of a power so inferior, that what He doeth is not what He Himself willeth; listen to what He also said, “As the Father raiseth up the dead and quickeneth them, even so the Son quickeneth whom He will.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so likewise, if in saying, “The Father that dwelleth in me, He doeth the works,” He is on that account not to be regarded as distinct in person from the Father, let us listen to His other words, “What things soever the Father doeth, these also doeth the Son likewise;” and He will be understood as speaking not of one person twice over, but of two who are one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But just because their mutual equality is such as not to interfere with their distinct personality, therefore He speaketh not of Himself, because He is not of Himself; and the Father also, that dwelleth in Him, Himself doeth the works, because He, by whom and with whom He doeth them, is not, save of [the Father] Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And then He goes on to say, “Believe ye not that I am in the Father, and the Father in me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or else believe me for the very works’ sake.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Formerly it was Philip only who was reproved, but now, it is shown that he was not the only one there that needed reproof.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For the very works’ sake,” He says, “believe ye that I am in the Father, and the Father in me:” for had we been separated, we should have been unable to do any kind of work inseparably.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what is this that follows?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that believeth on me, the works that I do shall he do also; and greater works than these shall he do; because I go unto my Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And whatsoever ye shall ask in my name, that will I do, that the Father may be glorified in the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If ye shall ask anything in my name, I will do it.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so He promised that He Himself would also do those greater works.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let not the servant exalt himself above his Lord, or the disciple above his Master.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He says that they will do greater works than He doeth Himself; but it is all by His doing such in or by them, and not as if they did them of themselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence the song that is addressed to Him, “I will love Thee, O Lord, my strength.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what, then, are those greater works?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it that their very shadow, as they themselves passed by, healed the sick?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is a mightier thing for a shadow, than for the hem of a garment, to possess the power of healing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The one work was done by Christ Himself, the other by them; and yet it was He that did both.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless, when He so spake, He was commending the efficacious power of His own words: for it was in this sense He had said, “The words that I speak unto you, I speak not of myself; but the Father that dwelleth in me, He doeth the works.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What works was He then referring to, but the words He was speaking?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They were hearing and believing, and their faith was the fruit of those very words: howbeit, when the disciples preached the gospel, it was not small numbers like themselves, but nations also that believed; and such, doubtless, are greater works.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet He said not, Greater works than these shall ye do, to lead us to suppose that it was only the apostles who would do so; for He added, “He that believeth on me, the works that I do shall he do also; and greater works than these shall he do.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is the case then so, that he that believeth on Christ doeth the same works as Christ, or even greater than He did?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Points like these are not to be treated in a cursory way, nor ought they to be hurriedly disposed of; and, therefore, as our present discourse must be brought to a close, we are obliged to defer their further consideration.
